title: LLM Decoding Mathematics
content:
# Interview Prep: Decoding Mathematics

## Q1: How does Top-P sampling vary the number of candidates dynamically compared to Top-K?

### Standard Answer
Top-K keeps the candidate set size constant (exactly $K$). In flat distributions, this can exclude likely candidates, whereas in peak distributions, it can include highly unlikely candidates. Top-P uses a cumulative probability threshold $P$. When the distribution is flat, many tokens are selected; when the distribution is narrow, only the top few are selected.
